name: Google Translate
description: Translate text using Google's Search translate widget (best-effort). Avoids the full Translate SPA selectors.

browser_automation.description: Hits google.com/search?q=translate+TEXT+to+LANG and reads the inline translation widget.

sequences.name: google_translate_extract
sequences.description: Translate input text to a target language and extract the translated string.

required_variables.name: to_language
required_variables.description: Target language. Either a name (Spanish, Japanese) or an ISO code (es, ja, fr, de).

steps.name: Navigate to Google Search translate widget
Extract translation (#tw-target-text is the canonical widget output)

help.summary: Translate a string to a target language using Google's inline translate widget.

parameters.name: text
parameters.description: Text to translate. Keep it short — the widget is meant for phrases/sentences, not paragraphs.

parameters.name: to_language
parameters.description: Target language NAME (Spanish, Japanese, French, German, Italian). Use the full name — ISO codes (es, ja) often fail to trigger Google's translate widget.

examples.command: rzn-browser run google translate --param text="good morning" --param to_language=Spanish
rzn-browser run google translate --param text="thank you" --param to_language=Japanese

help.returns: Object with translated_text (from #tw-target-text), source_text (from #tw-source-text), and search_text (full #search panel as fallback). If translated_text is empty, the widget didn't render — try a language NAME, not a code.
notes: Source language is auto-detected by Google.
For long-form translation or document upload, use the Translate SPA directly — this workflow only targets the Search widget.
